entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 27 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-27 04:11AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Censored and criticised, The Tenant of Wildfell Hall is perfect for our times
  • Wife's sexy surprise for husband backfires spectacularly
  • Frank Moorhouse obituary: From local views to big news

  • NRL great threatens to 'flog' opponent

  • Head knock rules Ponga out, Munster goes for scans

  • Calling all Millennials to the dance floor as Beyonce and Drake announce return of clubbing

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 27 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-27 03:24AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong-mainland China ties after 25 years: a chance for a reset or will underlying unease fester?

  • Hong Kong PLA garrison to be combat-ready for ‘toughest and most complicated’ situations amid security threats, commander vows
  • Rent control law fails to help Hong Kong’s poorest and leaves loopholes for landlords, advocacy group says

  • Coronavirus Hong Kong: hospitals experience uptick in Covid-related admissions as city logs more than 1,900 cases
  • Hong Kong not a ‘show flat for Western democracy, must win back Beijing’s trust with political security, avoiding harmful actions’
  • Hong Kong activist granted political asylum by Sweden in security law first for EU
  • Regina Ip is the right choice to steer Hong Kong’s comeback as Exco convenor
  • Chief of global airline body IATA warns no relief from sky-high airfares while oil prices remain elevated
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong frontline workers still waiting for Covid-19 subsidies because companies are refusing to apply
  • China questions the safety of open source code amid sanctions and tech dependency risks, but can it build a viable alternative?
  • Higher oil prices help Angola pay off debts to Chinese banks
  • Biden unveils US$600 billion global infrastructure plan at G7 summit to counter China’s Belt and Road
  • Heat turned up on China-Japan relationship with maritime disputes over islands and alliances
  • Coronavirus: China’s economic slowdown looks like a ‘lifetime of debt’ for some citizens
  • Under Xi Jinping China-Russia ties are defined by global interests and unity in opposition, report finds
  • Chinese authorities unveil plans to ‘maximise display of police force’ two weeks after group assault on women
  • Coronavirus: Chinese factory hub Shenzhen shuts down Hong Kong border district for 3 days
  • Australia hosts ‘historic’ Nasa launch – first from commercial site outside US
  • China’s education crackdown spurs Japan moves for international schools
  • Indonesia ‘democracy, human rights at risk’ as row over criminal code heats up
  • ‘We have nothing’ say survivors of deadly earthquake now facing disease outbreak
  • Netflix hit RRR by director SS Rajamouli, featuring Ram Charan, Jnr NTR, Ajay Devgn, shows India’s Telugu-language ‘Tollywood’ is no longer in Bollywood’s shadow
  • Ukraine war: Indonesia’s Jokowi says he will urge Putin to order immediate ceasefire during Moscow meeting
  • Pakistan loves chai so much, it may need to export green tea to fund its addiction
  • India’s cryptocurrency market was enjoying a ‘golden age’, but now it’s in a mess
  • UN drugs report: Cannabis use has risen with legalisation and Covid-19 lockdowns
  • Dozens arrested in Istanbul as LGBT marchers try to defy Pride ban
  • Suspect in Norway Pride Month shooting ‘refuses to talk to police’
  • Motown singer Diana Ross spreads the love at UK’s Glastonbury music festival
  • New York ‘duck sauce killer’ Glenn Hirsch could be freed on bail
  • Iran launches rocket into space as nuclear talks to resume
  • At least 21 teens die in mysterious circumstances in South Africa pub
  • Prince Charles’ US$3 million bags-of-cash claim, denies any wrongdoing
  • Pride parades march on with new urgency amid calls for changes in law
  • Designer perfumes hit by war and weather as vital ingredient becomes ‘liquid gold’
  • Russia bombs central Kyiv as world leaders gather in Europe for G7 summit
  • With Roe vs Wade overturned, fears grow online data could be weaponised against abortion seekers in US
  • Can Biden broker an Israel-Saudi Arabia normalisation deal during his Middle East trip?
  • Haidilao, Alibaba lead Hong Kong stocks to near three-month high on China recovery signals as Beijing eases lockdown
  • Hongkongers join bespoke Japan property tours to scout for investment opportunities amid yen’s slump
  • Taiwan stocks facing ‘big bully’ prompt Goldman retreat as cross-strait tensions inflict 20 to 40 per cent fund losses
  • US, Europe travel chaos a preview of the pain and indignities to come for Asia
  • Why central bank digital currencies would mean the end of data privacy
  • Chinese fintech giant Ant Group appoints risk-control veteran as executive in consumer lending arm amid restructuring
  • As leaders squabble, a world economy in dire danger is in need of a peacemaking hero
  • Stablecoin implosion shows it has ‘no role’ as a form of money, says Bank of International Settlements’ Asia chief
  • How parents can help their children get ready for a digital future
  • Actress Fala Chen on Irma Vep, Shang-Chi, parallels with Maggie Cheung and the Louis Vuitton catsuit she had to wear on set
  • School in China ditches sleeping on desks at nap time for health reasons and lets students set up their own tents to sleep in
  • Chicken, the ‘fowl of five virtues’ in Chinese lore, and why Singapore got into a flap over a shortage of the birds
  • How an old empty Hong Kong building became a homeless shelter and could serve as the template to get more people off the streets
  • From Zendaya to Chloe Grace Moretz, women in suits and ties are everywhere – as men change out of theirs
  • Organic cotton types, facts, certification and why you should buy clothes made from it
  • Summer holidays: all you need to know about Covid-19 measures, flights, what’s new and the best deals in Southeast Asia
  • Tailor went from sewing pants at 13 to dressing Princess Diana, Hollywood stars on Savile Row – ‘I’m living my dream,’ says Andrew Ramroop
  • Need a power nap? Have a cup of coffee first, experts say – you’ll feel refreshed and be less groggy when you wake up
  • ‘Everyone’s just bleeding’: unsold spring fashions in Shanghai pile up after lockdowns for Covid-19
  • Leslie Cheung or Mirror’s Keung To? Anita Mui or Tina Leung? Hong Kong fashion icons in 1997 vs 2022
  • 6 best cities for executive digital nomads: Miami, Lisbon, Dubai and Barcelona top Savills’ first ranking of locations ideal for long-term remote working
  • Inside South Indian star Nayanthara’s US$22 million net worth – the Netrikann actress lavishes husband Vignesh Shivan with gifts, boasts a huge real estate empire and gets around by private jet
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.
